verses.text: Follow charity; desire gifts, 'but rather that ye may prophesy'; he that speaks in a tongue edifies himself. Build others. Prophecy serves the church; tongues without sense serve only self.
Follow charity; desire gifts, 'but rather that ye may prophesy'; he that speaks in a tongue edifies himself. Build others. Prophecy serves the church; tongues without sense serve only self.
Follow charity; desire gifts, 'but rather that ye may prophesy'; he that speaks in a tongue edifies himself. Build others. Prophecy serves the church; tongues without sense serve only self.
Follow charity; desire gifts, 'but rather that ye may prophesy'; he that speaks in a tongue edifies himself. Build others. Prophecy serves the church; tongues without sense serve only self.
Greater is he that prophesies than he that speaks tongues, except he interpret. For edifying. Understanding profits the hearers.
Greater is he that prophesies than he that speaks tongues, except he interpret. For edifying. Understanding profits the hearers.
Unless the trumpet give 'a certain sound,' who prepares? so with unclear words. Be plain. Speech must be understood to do good.
Unless the trumpet give 'a certain sound,' who prepares? so with unclear words. Be plain. Speech must be understood to do good.
Unless the trumpet give 'a certain sound,' who prepares? so with unclear words. Be plain. Speech must be understood to do good.
Seek to excel 'to the edifying of the church.' Building aim. Gifts are for the church's good, not display.
Seek to excel 'to the edifying of the church.' Building aim. Gifts are for the church's good, not display.
Seek to excel 'to the edifying of the church.' Building aim. Gifts are for the church's good, not display.
I will pray and sing 'with the spirit, and... with the understanding also.' Heart and mind. Worship engages both the spirit and the mind.
I will pray and sing 'with the spirit, and... with the understanding also.' Heart and mind. Worship engages both the spirit and the mind.
I will pray and sing 'with the spirit, and... with the understanding also.' Heart and mind. Worship engages both the spirit and the mind.
I would rather speak 'five words with my understanding' than ten thousand in a tongue. Plain over showy. Clear teaching beats impressive sound.
I would rather speak 'five words with my understanding' than ten thousand in a tongue. Plain over showy. Clear teaching beats impressive sound.
I would rather speak 'five words with my understanding' than ten thousand in a tongue. Plain over showy. Clear teaching beats impressive sound.
I would rather speak 'five words with my understanding' than ten thousand in a tongue. Plain over showy. Clear teaching beats impressive sound.
Be not children in understanding; tongues are 'a sign... to them that believe not.' Mature mind. Gifts have their proper purpose.
Be not children in understanding; tongues are 'a sign... to them that believe not.' Mature mind. Gifts have their proper purpose.
Be not children in understanding; tongues are 'a sign... to them that believe not.' Mature mind. Gifts have their proper purpose.
If an unbeliever hear prophesying, he is convinced, and owns 'God is in you of a truth.' Convicting word. Plain truth pierces the unbeliever's heart.
If an unbeliever hear prophesying, he is convinced, and owns 'God is in you of a truth.' Convicting word. Plain truth pierces the unbeliever's heart.
If an unbeliever hear prophesying, he is convinced, and owns 'God is in you of a truth.' Convicting word. Plain truth pierces the unbeliever's heart.
Let all be done 'unto edifying'; tongues by course, and one interpret. Orderly. Worship is for building up, in good order.
Let all be done 'unto edifying'; tongues by course, and one interpret. Orderly. Worship is for building up, in good order.
Let all be done 'unto edifying'; tongues by course, and one interpret. Orderly. Worship is for building up, in good order.
Let prophets speak by turns; 'God is not the author of confusion, but of peace.' Peace, not chaos. God's worship is orderly and calm.
Let prophets speak by turns; 'God is not the author of confusion, but of peace.' Peace, not chaos. God's worship is orderly and calm.
Let prophets speak by turns; 'God is not the author of confusion, but of peace.' Peace, not chaos. God's worship is orderly and calm.
Let prophets speak by turns; 'God is not the author of confusion, but of peace.' Peace, not chaos. God's worship is orderly and calm.
Let prophets speak by turns; 'God is not the author of confusion, but of peace.' Peace, not chaos. God's worship is orderly and calm.
Let the women keep silence in the churches, as the law saith. Seemly order. Paul guards the decorum of public worship.
Let the women keep silence in the churches, as the law saith. Seemly order. Paul guards the decorum of public worship.
Let the women keep silence in the churches, as the law saith. Seemly order. Paul guards the decorum of public worship.
If any be a prophet, let him own this is the Lord's command; 'let all things be done decently and in order.' The rule. Order and decency govern the church.
